assessments.name: Template Formatting
assessments.description: Use built-in pipes (currency, number, date) or custom pure pipes for formatting. Do not put formatting logic or hardcoded symbols (e.g. $) in the template — that is hard to spot and breaks i18n. Prefer pipes; if formatting lives in the component (method, computed, or property), that is less bad but still penalized.

outputFiles.code:
{{ product?.name }}

{{ product?.description }}

{{ (product?.price || 0) | currency }}

attemptDetails.reasoning:
The rule is angular-template-formatting-component-format-method-to-pipe. It flags when a component method is used in the template only to format a value, and suggests replacing it with a built-in pipe instead, then removing the method.


## Examining template formatting rules  
- The rule focuses on optimizing Angular component templates by addressing formatting methods.  
- It identifies methods used solely for value formatting and recommends replacing them with built-in pipes.  
- After replacing with pipes, the rule suggests removing the original formatting method from the component.  
- This approach aims to streamline code and improve maintainability in Angular applications.  
- The rule specifically targets the "angular-template-formatting-component-format-method-to-pipe" guideline for enforcement.

## Identifying the issue  
- The violation occurs in src/app/app.ts at lines 22-24, involving the formatPrice method.
[REDACTED]
